title: Centre orders blocking of battery management apps used to turn off e-rickshaws
content:
Action comes after e-rickshaws in various parts of the country were turned off via apps developed
by Chinese firms to manage lithium ion battery packs; extortion incident reported in Ujjain

title: Decide plea against Dhruv Rathee’s video within 15 days: Delhi HC to Centre
content: The petitioner claims that the video contains statements that are “patently false, misleading and deliberately distorted” and capable of promoting communal disharmony

title: Engineers key to accelerating Kalyana Karnataka’s development: KKRDB Secretary
content: Two-day technical training programme inaugurated for government engineers from Bidar, Kalaburagi and Yadgir districts

title: PWD reforms to speed up projects in Kerala, says Minister P.K. Basheer
content: The department has decided to implement projects worth ₹513.14 crore as part of the State government’s 100-day action programme

title: Officials not obliged to attend ‘review’ meetings with Governor, says T.N. Law Minister
content: His remarks followed reports that Tamil Nadu Governor Rajendra Vishwanath Arlekar had wanted to hold a ‘review’ meeting with officials at the Madurai Circuit House

title: Ram temple donation theft: RSS calls for Hindu unity, seeks strict action against those guilty
content: RSS general secretary Dattatreya Hosabale cautioned against “conspiracies” by “anti-Hindu, anti-national forces” to exploit the incident and “malign Hinduism and society”

title: First Graduation ceremony of Bright B-School on Sunday
content: Bright Business School, Hubballi, has organised its first Graduation Day ‘Saraansh’ for its first batch of  students on Sunday

title: Erstwhile Mysore royal family forays into specialty coffee, unveils Berunda Coffee
content: Coffees being launched by granddaughter of the late Maharaja of Mysore Jayachamraja Wadiyar are named after palace’s Howdah-bearing elephants

title: BJP spokesperson condemns Minister’s comments on Governor
content: The Governor, as a Constitutional authority, has every right to draw attention to pressing civic and environmental problems affecting the people, says Narayanan Thirupathy

title: El Nino beckons Telangana Irrigation Minister to call for plan prioritising drinking water over irrigation
content: Uttam Kumar Reddy suggests farmers, in areas with less water availability, to opt for crops which need less water and can withstand heat instead of cultivating water-intensive crops

title: Supreme Court orders status quo on audit of Delhi discoms
content: The court posted the case for a further hearing on July 15. Meanwhile, the apex court’s status quo order would effectively put on hold the original DERC order to have the Comptroller and Auditor General (CAG) audit the discoms

title: Ammonia gas leak: Death toll rises to 18
content: Currently, 14 women are undergoing treatment – one each on ventilator support and oxygen support, and 12 stable

title: Wayanad gets special healthcare package
content: Minister announces a dozen projects, including a superspeciality hospital and mobile medical units for areas affected by human-wildlife conflict

title: Kannur Corporation plans to launch online booking system for septic tank cleaning
content: The service will be available on demand to all residents within the jurisdiction of the Corporation, including apartments and other residential complexes that require septic tank cleaning, says the Mayor

title: Ram temple donation ‘theft’: RJD MP moves Supreme Court seeking audit of temple trust finances
content: Plea in court says it is of ‘exceptional public importance’ to safeguard the administration of the Trust and protect the offerings made by millions of devotees; seeks CBI probe into allegations of embezzlement of donations to the Ram Temple

title: SIT likely to re-audit Ram Temple Trust accounts for last five years
content: Repeat audit likely to scrutinise trust’s accounts over a five year period, covering construction-related expenditure as well as donations; SIT members speak with people at Ram Temple on July 3

title: Congress accuses Modi govt. of ‘calibrated capitulation’ to China
content: The world’s largest hydroelectric project in Medog continues, imperilling India’s water security in relation to the Brahmaputra, says Jairam Ramesh

title: ECI has discretion to announce bypolls any time within six months of seat falling vacant, experts say
content: At lease 14 Assembly seats, six Parliamentary seats stay vacant as ECI announces bypolls in only three; Congress slams ECI’s hurry to schedule Datia bypoll despite ongoing case over MLA’s disqualification;  in Milkipur, the Opposition accused the ECI of “delay” tactics to benefit the BJP

title: We will factor all related developments in overall approach to Teesta issue: MEA
content: After Bangladesh PM Tarique Rahman’s maiden visit to China, India says its development assistance to projects in Bangladesh work on the basis of a ‘mutually agreed roadmap’

title: Bengaluru creche abuse case: Caregiver arrested, more arrests likely
content: The case came to light after purported videos showing the alleged mistreatment of children at the creche, Little Buds Day Care, surfaced on social media

title: Tragedy Strikes Mumbai, Delhi's Long-Delayed Tree Census Still Stuck
content: Officials say the census will create a scientific baseline for Delhi's urban trees, helping authorities monitor tree health, detect illegal felling, plan plantation drives and improve conservation planning

title: On Usha Vance's "Book-Reading" Podcast, Trump Discusses Past Presidents
content: As Trump turned through the children's book, he made observations about past presidents, cracked some jokes, and threw in a plug for the extensive ballroom he's building on the White House grounds.

title: Trump And Netanyahu Hold Phone Call, Agree To Meet "Soon"
content: The conversation comes after the United States and Israel fought alongside each other in their military campaign against Iran.

title: Weather Highlights: Many Parts Of Gujarat Flooded After Rain, Schools Declare Holiday Near Mumbai
content: Strong westerly winds likely to prevail over the southern peninsular India and central India till July 8, the India Meteorological Department said.

title: Cross-Border Handlers, Propaganda: Inside Jaish Module Busted In Gujarat
content: The background of the case involves a massive joint operation where five special Anti-Terror Squad (ATS) teams collaborated with regional police forces.

title: Russia Jails 12 'Neo-Nazi' Group Members For Planning To Kill Media Mogul
content: Among the other crimes of which the 12 were accused were attacks on migrants and LGBTQ people, as well as committing robberies and inciting ethnic hatred.

title: Bengaluru Daycare Abuse Whistleblower Questioned By Cops, Family Raises Alarm
content: The development comes after police on Friday arrested two people in connection with the case as the probe into the alleged abuse gathered pace.

title: Jaish Module Translated Material Into Gujarati To Expand Network In State
content: To maximise the propagation and dissemination of the outfit's extremist ideology, the operatives were systematically translating radical literature from Urdu into the local Gujarati language.

title: Putin Says Fuel Shortage "Not Critical" As Russia Ramps Up Attack On Ukraine
content: The Russian leader appears to believe his government can keep the fuel crisis from eroding his authority and support for the war he launched

title: Thousands Dead, Records Broken: All You Need To Know About Europe's Heatwave
content: World Weather Attribution, a network of climate scientists, said the heatwave was the "most severe ever recorded" based on a three-day forecast of average peak temperatures over the region studied.

title: Trinamool vs Trinamool As Rebel Faction 'Takes Control' Of Party Headquarters
content: The rebels also put up a poster on the front gate identifying senior MLA Arup Roy as chairman of the All India Trinamool Congress. Photographs and cutouts of Mamata Banerjee in the building were left in place.

title: Tamil Nadu Unveils Rural Contracts Policy To Boost Competition, Create Jobs
content: The TVK government seeks to translate its electoral promise of expanding employment and entrepreneurship opportunities into administrative reforms

title: Delhi Police Arrest Man, Recover 59 Rolls Of Banned Chinese Manja
content: Delhi Police have arrested a 27-year-old man and recovered 59 rolls (charkhis) of banned Chinese manja from his possession during a raid in northeast Delhi's Seelampur, officials said on Friday.

title: No Minister For 26 Years, Karnataka's Kodavas Seek Cabinet Berth
content: A delegation argued that Kodagu has remained without representation in the Karnataka cabinet for over two decades, citing that the district last had ministerial representation in 1999 during the Congress government led by SM Krishna

title: 'Digital Propaganda On App': How Jaish Planned To Spread Terror In Gujarat
content: The network positioned its members across several regional hubs, heavily utilising local madrasas to run their operations and extend dawat, or invitations, to join the terror group.

title: Man Who Killed Wife Gets Reduced Sentence Over Her '1,000 Husbands' Remark
content: A Division Bench of Justice Vivek Agarwal and Justice Avanindra Kumar Singh partly allowed the appeal filed by Shiva, who had been convicted by a Chhindwara trial court for killing his pregnant wife, Kiran.

title: Domestic Help Thought No One Would Notice, Instagram Reel Exposes Her Theft
content: Nitisha Vats, who lives in Sikka Kimaya Greens in Dehradun's IT Park, reported to police that the theft took place about a year ago when she was residing in Doon Republic Apartments in Bhagirathi Puram in Jakhan.

title: Maharashtra Bans Sale Of Energy Drinks Within 500 Metres Of Schools
content: In a serious bid to regulate the consumption of high-caffeine beverages among youth, the Maharashtra government on Friday announced a comprehensive ban on the sale of high energy drinks like Sting within a 500-metre radius of schools.

title: Assam 1st In India To Commercially Produce Matcha Tea, Sold For Rs 3,000 A Kg
content: The first commercially produced batch of Matcha tea was sold at the Guwahati Tea Auction Centre for Rs 3,000, reflecting encouraging market interest in the premium product.

title: Provident Fund Over Rs 1,800 Optional: Why It's Hard to Ignore 8.25% Returns
content: Provident Funds have been one of the most reliable retirement plans for Indians. Given its low-risk and tax-free returns, the instrument is widely famous across the working class.

title: Codewords, nicknames, emojis: Cops to decode Siya Goyal's 'secret chats' with Chetan
content: Pune police investigating realtor Ketan Agarwal's murder recovered coded chats from accused Siyal Goyal's phone, decipherable only by her. Despite seeking extended custody to decode the messages, the court remanded Goyal and her alleged lover, Chetan Chaudhary, to judicial custody. Investigators are also examining financial transactions and witness statements, asserting evidence points to murder.

title: UK Hindus lose land bid to church and Muslim group
content: British Hindus in Cambridgeshire have been denied their first place of worship in Northstowe, with a local council awarding faith land to a church and Muslim group. Hindu Samaj Northstowe's bid, proposing an interfaith centre and temple, was scored lower than the Northstowe Church Network's proposal. This leaves around 150 Hindu families without a local space for worship and cultural practices, forcing them to travel long distances.

title: Ram temple theft deeply hurt devotees, don't let anti-Hindu forces exploit row: RSS
content: The RSS has expressed deep hurt over the Ayodhya Ram temple donation theft, urging the Shri Ram Janmabhoomi Teerth Kshetra Trust to address management deficiencies.  Appealing for patience and restraint, the organization warned against anti-Hindu forces exploiting the controversy.  The incident has sparked scrutiny over temple finances and security, with authorities launching a probe.  The RSS emphasized the need for transparency and accountability to maintain devotees' faith.

title: 'Going to have ups and downs': England star issues reality check for Sooryavanshi
content: The cricket world has been waiting for Sooryavanshi's India debut ever since he was picked for the tour of the UK following his performances for Rajasthan Royals in IPL 2026.

title: 3 Delhi projects you paid for, but can’t use: Admin delays deny public use of key facilities
content: Several completed public facilities in Delhi, including Karol Bagh's Heritage Park and a Jangpura pet park, remain inaccessible to citizens years after construction. Bureaucratic inertia and procedural delays, even for projects with no financial constraints like the JPN Library, are preventing these valuable assets from serving the public, leading to significant underutilization of investment.

title: After years of tracking packages, Amazon planning to use the same playbook for employees
content: Amazon is reportedly deploying wearable devices to precisely track warehouse employees in support roles, previously monitored manually. This new 'Right Station Link' program aims to automate data collection, eliminate human error, and optimize worker placement, potentially saving billions. The move follows Amazon's success in tracking frontline workers and addresses concerns about overstaffing in mobile positions.

title: India’s women-owned business map has a western tilt
content: Women's entrepreneurship is growing in India, but unevenly. Cities in Gujarat and Maharashtra lead in female-owned businesses, while northern cities lag. This disparity highlights the complex interplay of economic structure, local conditions, and societal norms. While government initiatives are noted, deeper empowerment hinges on grassroots changes within homes and local markets, suggesting a patchwork of progress rather than a uniform national trend.

title: Congress demands dissolution of Ram temple trust
content: The Congress party has called for the dissolution of the Ram Janmabhoomi Teerth Kshetra Trust amidst allegations of donation theft. They are demanding a Supreme Court-monitored investigation and urged Prime Minister Narendra Modi to address the controversy.  The party criticized the RSS's "clean chit" as shameful and highlighted public anger over the alleged misappropriation of funds.

title: 'Criminal trespassers': TMC vows court battle as rival faction captures party HQ
content: The Trinamool Congress (TMC) has accused a rival faction led by expelled MLA Ritabrata Banerjee of illegally seizing its Kolkata headquarters, labeling them 'criminal trespassers' and vowing legal action. The party claims police allowed the rebels to lock the premises, alleging a conspiracy with the BJP. This follows the rebel group's move to stake claim over the TMC's name and symbol with the Election Commission.

title: Days after Meta Layoffs, Zuckerberg says companies should empower workers, not replace them
content: Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg believes AI-driven job losses are exaggerated, advocating for tools that boost individual productivity. This comes as the tech giant laid off 8,000 employees, reassigning another 7,000 to AI projects, while scrapping thousands of open roles.  The company is heavily investing in AI infrastructure, aiming for efficiency amidst a competitive landscape.  This stance contrasts with other industry leaders' more cautious outlooks on AI's impact on employment.

title: Govt may look at a separate legal framework for AI: Secretary
content: India is considering a dedicated legal framework for Artificial Intelligence, a significant departure from its previous stance of using existing laws. The Ministry of Electronics and IT believes the time is right to draft specific AI legislation. This move signals a potential shift in the government's approach to regulating the rapidly evolving technology, aiming to balance innovation with necessary oversight.

title: Scientists find butterflies that live nearly a year and barely age
content: Recent research reveals that tropical butterflies known as Heliconius exhibit remarkable longevity, surviving nearly a year with little deterioration, unlike their transient relatives. This species showcases preserved strength and body mass, indicative of a slower aging process. Their unique pollen-rich diet aids in this phenomenon, alongside intrinsic biological factors. This discovery may serve as a groundbreaking reference for exploring the principles of healthy aging and longevity in nature.

title: Archaeologists find 5,000-year-old ritual circle under Scottish island
content: Beneath the lush peat of Scotland's Isle of Arran, a 5,000-year-old ritual circle has emerged, thanks to innovative geophysical scanning. This discovery reveals a ring of 12 underground pits, likely from the Neolithic or Bronze Age, without the disturbance of digging. The site adds a mysterious new chapter to the storied past of Machrie Moor, potentially indicating the presence of ancient ceremonial timber structures.

title: Sergey Brin sold his NYC real estate: Here's what Mamdani's rent freeze cost him
content: Google co-founder Sergey Brin has divested his stake in a New York City real estate fund for a fraction of its value, selling for just six cents on the dollar. This significant loss comes as Mayor Zohran Mamdani's rent freeze tightens conditions for landlords. The fund, A&E Real Estate, confirmed the sale, citing the challenging market for rent-stabilized apartments.

title: Govt orders Apple, Google to remove 7 Chinese apps
content: Authorities have directed Google and Apple to remove seven Chinese battery management apps, including BAT-BMS, from their stores. These apps were reportedly misused to remotely disable battery-operated vehicles, such as e-rickshaws, causing significant disruption for drivers. This marks the first government action addressing data security and remote interference concerns linked to vehicle devices, with further app bans anticipated if similar issues arise.

title: Jets' $68M star linked to Sabres, Hurricanes in blockbuster trade buzz
content: The Winnipeg Jets signing of Stuart Skinner has set off rumors on the future of the franchise’s goaltender, Connor Hellebuyck. There are no trade talks confirmed, but league observers believe the move gives Winnipeg more room to maneuver should the organization decide to make significant roster changes this NHL offseason.

title: Usain Bolt, Kasi Bennett's 2026 net worth: Earnings, endorsements and lifestyle
content: Nine years post-retirement, Usain Bolt's global icon status fuels a $90 million fortune, bolstered by enduring endorsements and business ventures. His partner, Kasi Bennett, independently contributes between $1-2 million through her entrepreneurial and philanthropic work. Together, this Jamaican power couple boasts a combined net worth nearing $92 million, showcasing a successful blend of family, luxury, and expanding business interests.

title: Bruins veteran reportedly blocked Darnell Nurse trade using no-trade clause
content: Darnell Nurse was involved in trade talks that extended past the eventual deal to the San Jose Sharks, according to reports. A new report says the Edmonton Oilers were talking trade with the Boston Bruins until a player's contract protection got in the way. The development adds a new chapter to one of the most talked-about trade stories in the NHL this offseason.

title: Amarnath Yatra 2026: Second batch of 3,865 pilgrims leave Jammu
content: The second batch of pilgrims has commenced their journey to the Shri Amarnath Ji shrine. Authorities have issued a strict advisory, emphasizing that only registered individuals will be permitted to proceed.  Thousands of pilgrims arriving without prior registration are urged to wait for their assigned travel dates, as tatkal registration slots are severely limited. Security escorts are in place for both the Baltal and Pahalgam routes.

title: Agra horror: Wife feeds man poisoned kheer, buries him under bathroom floor
content: A shocking crime has surfaced in Agra where a wife allegedly murdered her husband, burying his body beneath their bathroom floor. For nearly 45 days, she maintained the facade of his disappearance, feigning grief. A routine police verification, coupled with the victim's brother's suspicions, led to the chilling discovery and the wife's confession. The body was exhumed from beneath the cemented floor.

title: Child slams J&K education minister in video, sparks outcry
content: A viral video of a 12-year-old criticizing J&K Education Minister Sakina Itoo over delayed summer vacations has sparked controversy. Kashmir's chief cleric deemed the child's remarks impolite, while the Child Welfare Committee summoned the news portal for uploading the clip without parental consent. The CWC also ordered the video's removal and an FIR, citing concerns for the child's privacy and well-being.

title: Centre ready for elected UT body, Article 371-like safeguards, say Ladakh groups
content: Ladakh's political groups and Union home ministry officials held a positive "informal" meeting, finalizing draft minutes of previous talks. A UT-level elected body with significant powers will be established, while statehood remains a long-term aspiration. Discussions also covered customized constitutional safeguards for Ladakh, with both sides expressing optimism about addressing regional concerns and reaching a swift conclusion.

title: JCO injured in landmine blast in J&K's Rajouri
content: A Junior Commissioned Officer was injured in a landmine blast during a routine patrol along the Line of Control in Rajouri district. Naib Subedar Hiteshwar Gogoi of the 14 Assam Regiment was rushed for medical treatment. Such incidents are attributed to displaced mines, with recent similar accidents involving Army personnel in the region.

title: Pitch invader steals Rahmanullah Gurbaz's cap mid-match & runs away - Watch
content: The incident took place during the Shpageeza Cricket League match between Band-e-Amir Dragons and Mis Ainak Knights on Friday.

title: No engine damage: Govt issues 10-point clarification on E20 ethanol blending programme
content: Government has strongly refuted social media misinformation regarding India's E20 ethanol blending program.  Extensive testing confirms no significant adverse effects on vehicles, engines, or fuel safety.  Claims about excessive water use and environmental risks are also debunked, highlighting the program's benefits in foreign exchange savings and farmer payments.  The initiative is presented as scientifically backed and globally adopted.

title: Orlando Gill's World Cup dream comes full circle with heartfelt jersey return
content: Orlando Gill’s World Cup journey is about much more than football. The Paraguay goalkeeper once sold his Under-20 jersey to help pay medical bills for his newborn son. After Paraguay defeated Germany, the man who bought that jersey offered to return it free of charge. Gill now heads into the France match carrying both World Cup hopes and a deeply personal dream.

title: Oil slips to pre-Iran war levels: Is a petrol, diesel price cut coming?
content: Petrol and diesel prices in India have risen by around Rs 7.5 per litre since May 15. The first hike in the retail rates came over two and a half months after the US-Iran war began. Private retailer Nayara Energy has already cut rates of petrol and diesel.

title: 'Why are the police here?' Wife's chilling remark after killing husband of nearly 30 years
content: A woman has been sentenced to life imprisonment for murdering her husband of nearly 30 years, with a minimum of 12 years before parole eligibility. The court rejected her claim that the fatal stabbing was an accident, finding she deliberately attacked him during an altercation. Body-camera footage released by police shows her appearing confused and questioning why officers were present, stating, "You don't think I've murdered him, do you?"

title: 'It's not straightforward': India coach's clear message on Sooryavanshi debut
content: India's bowling coach Morne Morkel affirmed continued backing for openers Sanju Samson and Abhishek Sharma, despite calls for 15-year-old Vaibhav Sooryavanshi's debut. Morkel emphasised faith in proven performers and avoiding disrupting batting order. He praised Sooryavanshi's smooth integration and potential readiness for future opportunities, while also commending pacers Prince Yadav and Harshit Rana for their impressive performances.

title: Self-immolation outside UN draws fresh attention to Tibet
content: A Tibetan activist's self-immolation outside the UN headquarters in New York has reignited global attention on Tibet's human rights crisis. Lobga Rangzen's desperate act, protesting Chinese rule and new assimilation laws, highlights the fading international focus on the issue. This tragic event, occurring after over 150 similar protests in Tibet, challenges governments to confront the unresolved situation, forcing a difficult conversation amidst strained US-China relations.

title: Jason Kelce and Taylor Swift’s fiance Travis Kelce interview Prince William
content: In a surprising move, Prince William appeared on the New Heights podcast. This diverts the attention from the highly anticipated Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce wedding. The interview features a grand introduction for the prince. It probably promises to reveal details and potentially shift focus from wedding. Did Prince William plat cupid? Because NFL sportscaster Erin Andrews is credited as a cupid in the couple’s romance. To top it, she was present at their wedding rehearsal dinner.

title: 'Fastest man alive' Usain Bolt turns up for Djokovic's Wimbledon clash - Watch
content: Videos and pictures of Bolt watching the match quickly spread across social media. Even years after retiring from athletics, the eight-time Olympic gold medallist continues to draw attention wherever he goes.

title: Badrinath-Kedarnath Temple Committee orders inquiry after claims of donation theft
content: Allegations of misappropriated donations at Badrinath Dham have prompted an inquiry by the Shri Badrinath-Kedarnath Temple Committee. Chairman Hemant Dwivedi confirmed an investigation is underway, with explanations sought from staff. Strict action will follow if guilt is established. This follows similar scrutiny at the Ayodhya temple, where financial irregularities are being probed.

title: How a cricket technology saved Ronaldo's FIFA World Cup dream - Explained
content: Portugal secured victory through Ronaldo's penalty and a stoppage-time header from Goncalo Ramos. But the biggest talking point came in the closing minutes when Croatia had a late equaliser ruled out after a VAR review.

title: PM Modi shares glimpse of new Jodhpur airport ahead of inauguration - see pics
content: Prime Minister Narendra Modi inaugurated the new Jodhpur airport terminal, a significant boost for tourism and connectivity, blending modern design with Rajasthani heritage. He also launched the Modified UDAN scheme from Jodhpur, aiming to enhance regional air connectivity nationwide by developing 100 aerodromes and supporting airline operations. This infrastructure push is set to invigorate commerce and travel in the region.

title: TMC vs TMC: Ritabrata faction takes control of party HQ as rebellion intensifies
content: A significant power struggle has erupted within the Trinamool Congress as a rebel faction, led by Ritabrata Banerjee, seized control of the party's Kolkata headquarters. This move follows their appeal to the Election Commission to claim the party's name and symbol. The faction asserts its legitimacy amidst ongoing disputes with the Mamata Banerjee-led camp, escalating the internal conflict over organisational leadership.

title: Customised model suitable for Ladakh will be adopted under Article 371, says Home Ministry draft
content: The Ministry releases the minutes of meeting agreed upon by Centre and leaders of civil society groups of Leh Apex Body and Kargil Democratic Alliance

title: Amarnath yatra begins, PM Modi urges pilgrims to keep route clean
content: Prime Minister Narendra Modi urged devotees to make the journey a symbol of cleanliness, safety, environmental protection, and support for local livelihoods and nation-building

title: Tamil Nadu govt. fixes issues with non-subdivision online patta transfers
content: Under the modified rules, auto-mutation will happen during land sale when the landowner and his family members co-sign, says  Minister for Revenue and Disaster Management K.A.  Sengottaiyan

title: DMK MLA Anitha Radhakrishnan arrested over remarks against Tamil Nadu CM Vijay
content: Madras High Court dismisses his anticipatory bail plea; he later alleges that he was pressured to join TVK

title: 75th anniversary of India-Japan diplomatic ties to be celebrated with cricket, art, anime events in 2027
content: The two governments, in cooperation with their respective stakeholders, will celebrate 2027 as the 'India-Japan Year of Shared Horizons', the Ministry of External Affairs announced on Thursday

title: IWT will be in abeyance till Pakistan irrevocably stops sponsoring cross-border terrorism: Govt.
content: Earlier this week, Pakistan organised a conference on the Indus and water sharing where the country’s Foreign Minister Ishaq Dar referred to the IWT as vital for “regional peace, stability and cooperation”.

title: Amit Shah to review Manipur security situation on July 4; CM likely to visit Churachandpur, a first since 2023 violence
content: Mr. Shah will hold the meeting with Manipur government’s Security Adviser Kuldiep Singh and State Director General of Police Mukesh Singh, Director General of Military Operations (DGMO) and D-Gs of Assam Rifles, Central Reserve Police Force (CRPF) and Border Security Force (BSF); CM Yumnam Khemchand Singh is not part of Saturday’s meeting

title: Now, you can update email on Aadhaar app
content:
NEW DELHI: Aadhaar holders can now add or update their email ID directly through the Aadhaar app, with UIDAI making the service free for six months from July 1. The facility removes the need to visit an Aadhaar centre for email updates. UIDAI said more than 2.5 lakh people updated their email address in the first two days of the feature going live. Linking an email ID with Aadhaar will help residents receive real-time alerts whenever an authentication request is carried out. UIDAI said this would improve transparency and provide an additional layer of security by allowing Aadhaar number holders to track the use of their identity details.

End of Article

title: SC critical of HC order but refuses to stay Sonam bail
content:
NEW DELHI: Supreme Court Friday said it was prima facie of the view that Meghalaya HC should not have granted bail on technical grounds to Sonam Raghuvanshi, who is accused of killing her husband during their honeymoon, but refrained from staying the order after it was informed that she was already out of jail. A bench of Justices M M Sundresh and Sheel Nagu expressed reservation with the HC's judgment but did not pass a stay order and issued notice on a plea by Meghalaya govt seeking cancellation of bail. Solicitor general Tushar Mehta and the state's advocate general, Amit Kumar, urged SC to stay the order, saying three bail applications were rejected on merit. Sonam got bail on the fourth attempt on a typographical error, as cops had erroneously referred to BNS Section 403(1) instead of Section 103(1). When Sonam's counsel told the bench she was already out on bail, the court said, "If she is released, then we cannot stay the order ."

End of Article
